chapter_context.summary: Features the second Servant Song, where the Servant is called to be a light to the Gentiles, and Zion is passionately reassured of God's unforgettable love.

outline.text: Second Servant Song: Servant Anointed to Restore Tribes
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Second Servant Song: Servant Anointed to Restore Tribes (49:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Day of Salvation and Return of Captives with Singing
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Day of Salvation and Return of Captives with Singing (49:8-13), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.
This section focuses on Zion's Unforgettable Love: Children Restored (49:14-26), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Comfort and Redemption
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of comfort and redemption, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 49, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
